description_long: This object is a classic stovetop moka pot coffee maker, composed of three main sections: a lower chamber for water, a middle funnel-shaped filter for ground coffee, and an upper chamber for the brewed coffee. The body is predominantly metallic with a matte black finish, and the handle is ergonomically shaped and made of a heat-resistant material, likely plastic or bakelite. The lid is hinged and can be opened, as shown, with a small knob for easy lifting. The spout is integrated into the upper chamber for pouring, and the overall form is angular with faceted sides. The object is shown in an exploded view, revealing the internal filter basket and the separation between the water and coffee chambers. No visible branding or control panels are present, and the design is utilitarian, suitable for manual brewing on a stovetop.
description: A matte black stovetop moka pot coffee maker with a faceted metal body, hinged lid, and ergonomic handle, shown in an exploded view revealing its internal filter basket.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing the open lid, handle, and spout, with all three main sections separated vertically.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the handle and the open lid, with the filter basket and chambers aligned.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the profile of the handle, open lid, and the separation of the three main parts.
description_view_3: Direct rear view focusing on the back of the upper chamber and handle, with the open lid and filter basket visible.
